# QC-10: SLA/SLO Monitoring

**Track:** Quality Controls (`QC`) · **Priority:** COULD (optional) · **Status:** draft

Continuously measure all defined Service Level Indicators (SLIs) against Service Level Objectives (SLOs): availability, latency percentiles, error rates, and throughput. Track error budget consumption rates and alert when burn rate exceeds defined thresholds before SLO breach occurs.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 6 | Observability & Maintenance | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Continuously measure SLIs; compute error budget consumption; trigger alerts when burn rate thresholds are exceeded. |
| **Human does** | Respond to alerts; make decisions on service degradation; initiate feedback loop if code change is needed. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| SLO monitoring dashboard | evidence | Continuous record of SLI measurements, SLO compliance status, and error budget consumption. | 2 years operational + 7 years regulatory |
| SLO breach alert | alert | Real-time alert when error budget burn rate exceeds threshold or SLO is breached. |  |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | continuous |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | escalate |
| **Escalate to** | SRE Lead |

SLO breach triggers immediate escalation and may initiate a feedback loop for code remediation.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 10 | Detection of anomalous activities impacting operational performance |
| DORA | Art. 8(4) | Operational resilience monitoring of ICT systems |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Engineering / SRE · **Owner role:** SRE Lead
